wand_woods.name: Willow
wand_woods.description: Can heal one heavy wound of any type by physically touching the target (once per day)

wand_woods.name: Apple
wand_woods.description: Can calm (render non-aggressive) any beast under size 3 by physically touching it with one's wand (once per day; You may have to perform an agility check against one of the creatures movement abilities).

holdable_items.name: Kakaw Tzolkʼin Disc
holdable_items.description: A palm‑sized jade disc inlaid with obsidian glyphs that echo the Mayan Tzolkʼin calendar. When ground cacao is placed on its surface and the glyphs are traced in sequence, the disc briefly reveals auspicious signs for the next 260 minutes, granting its bearer heightened intuition for ritual timing.

general_items.name: Boomberry Bomb
general_items.description: These bombs will deal one medium wound of explosive damage to anyone nearby when they explode. The bombs are stable and can be handled by anyone.

general_items.name: Muggle Binoculars
general_items.description: A generic pair of muggle binoculars

general_items.name: Muggle Lockpicks
general_items.description: These muggle lockpicks can be used to attempt to open only muggle locks.

general_items.name: Muggle Compass
general_items.description: A general muggle compass.

general_items.name: Muggle Magnifying Glass
general_items.description: General muggle magnifying glass

general_items.name: Muggle Camera
general_items.description: This muggle camera will not work in areas with many magical beings or any magical locations. Incidentally, this cannot be used to photograph magical artefacts, items, wands, creatures, plants, or anything magical. The camera simply records a blurry picture of a field.

general_items.name: Lute
general_items.description: A musical instruments

general_items.name: Worry dolls
general_items.description:
Typically found in Central America, these worry dolls serve two purposes. First, they can reduce anxiety-related damage by 2 heavy wounds (one time per day). 
Second, they can be burned to help an individual solve a problem. The individual must state what problem they have, then burn the doll. The individual will then receive +5 bonuses to any direct attempts to solve that specific problem for the next 24 hours. The Headmaster may provide additional bonuses or hints at their discretion.

general_items.name: Harp
general_items.description: Musical Instrument

general_items.name: Crwth
general_items.description: Welsh musical instrument

general_items.name: Pibgorn
general_items.description: Welsh instrument

general_items.name: Copper Filings
general_items.description: Simple copper filings

general_items.name: Drum
general_items.description: A simple musical drum

general_items.name: Magical Dowsing Rod
general_items.description: These can be used in divination, specifically dowsing

general_items.name: Clear Quartz
general_items.description: A crystalline mineral.

general_items.name: Babylonian Threshold Figurine
general_items.description: These figurines, when buried at a threshold, can guard against evil spirits entering. Set value 15.

general_items.name: Egyptian Amulet
general_items.description: These amulets serve as protection against disease. Anyone wearing one will be protected (set value 15) from any attempt to disease or poison the wearer.

general_items.name: Curse Tablet
general_items.description: A curse or evil desire is written on this tablet. This tablet then emits that curse or desire upon its intended victims when they are in proximity. Some tablets may target others indiscriminately.

general_items.name: Morning Dew
general_items.description: The dew collected from plants in the morning

general_items.name: Cursed Fecundity Figure
general_items.description: These ancient figures can grant significantly improved fertility. Those struggling to conceive may place one of these figures near the site of attempted conception and will experience near certain success of conception. However, these cursed figures produce children that are much much more likely to turn toward darkness. The true nature of the figure (whether cursed or not) is deeply concealed. An arithmancy roll of 25 is required to detect whether a figure is cursed or not. Because of the risk, many have abandoned their use.

general_items.name: Lunar Caustic
general_items.description: Moon-like, silvery appearance with caustic properties.

general_items.name: Alchemical Salt
general_items.description: A form of salt that is purified and operates as a means of preparing materials for transmutation.

general_items.name: Distilled Water
general_items.description: A purified form of water that serves as a base for the elixir.

general_items.name: Philosopher's Wool
general_items.description: Also known as nix alba or white snow, this wool helps to whiten many materials.

general_items.name: Lime Water
general_items.description: Water with lime in it! Some believe the slight acidity aids in the preparation of materials.

general_items.name: Crushed Eggshells
general_items.description: These crushed eggshells are crucial in the basic phases of Alchemy.

general_items.name: Vitriol
general_items.description: Represents several crystals

general_items.name: Litharge
general_items.description: A catalyst in many alchemical reactions.

general_items.name: Calcined Magnesite
general_items.description: A materials oft used in Alchemy.

general_items.name: Moonstone
general_items.description: Moonstone

general_items.name: Basalt
general_items.description: A common Alchemical rock.

general_items.name: Flint
general_items.description: An alchemical rock

general_items.name: Mica
general_items.description: A mineral known for its flaky, airy nature, mica is used to enhance the user’s connection to air and wind.

general_items.name: Gold Leaf
general_items.description: A thin sheet of gold

general_items.name: Lunar Water
general_items.description: Water collected from rain puddles soon after the ending of the storm. Full moon must be visible at the time of collection. Dirt/mud contaminate this water easily so it usually needs to be collected from a clean vessel.

general_items.name: Iron Filings
general_items.description: Small filings of Iron ore

general_items.name: Realgar
general_items.description: Represents a single crystal

general_items.name: Gypsum
general_items.description: This is a soft mineral.

general_items.name: Quicklime
general_items.description: Reacts with moisture to create a temporary soupy/pasty substance that (on its own) doesn't seem to have much purpose.

general_items.name: Mineral-Rich Spring Water
general_items.description: Naturally occurring spring water that has been infused with a variety of minerals. This water not only acts as a catalyst for the transmutation process but also imbues the elixir with a natural affinity for stone and earth, enhancing its effectiveness in manipulating these elements.

general_items.name: Silvered Athame
general_items.description:
A ceremonial blade, usually double-edged, used in various pagan and magical rituals to direct energy and maintain the sanctity of one's inner circle.

Can be used as a melee weapon rolling agility. Causes 2L Wounds of cutting damage if successful. 1M wound for critical successes.

general_items.name: Crystal Phials
general_items.description: These specialized phials grant additional benefits to the user when concocting potions.

general_items.name: Demure Wristcuff
general_items.description: A refined and subtle accessory that combines elegance with understated charm.

general_items.name: Cinnabar
general_items.description: Represents a small crystal

general_items.name: Aqua Fortis
general_items.description: AKA Strong water. This powerful acid is used by alchemists in various processes. It can destroy some alchemical metals and may be capable of degrading certain magical barriers.

general_items.name: Simple Aegis Talisman
general_items.description: This talisman often offers basic a sense of internal fortitude and resistance.

general_items.name: Delicately Carved Sardonyx Stone
general_items.description: A Sardonyx stone engraved with the image of an eagle.

general_items.name: Gilded Wristwatch
general_items.description: This subtle, shiny wristwatch adds to your dashing nature.

general_items.name: Rusty Monocle
general_items.description: An aged monocle offering a glimpse into the past.

general_items.name: Worn Quill
general_items.description: A faded quill that aids your reasoning.

general_items.name: Abramelin Square
general_items.description: An Abramelin Square is a grid of numbers arranged so that the sum of the numbers in each row, column, and diagonal is the same

general_items.name: Hex Signet
general_items.description: A small, circular amulet painted with intricate folk patterns.

general_items.name: Hag Stone
general_items.description: Also known as a witch stone, with naturally occurring holes, worn to protect against evil.

general_items.name: Altaic Rune Stones
general_items.description: Altaic Rune Stones are ancient artifacts engraved with Turkic runic scripts, used by Turkic and Mongolian tribes for divination, protection, and commemorative purposes. Made from stone or bone, these symbols bridged the earthly and spiritual realms in shamanistic rituals, marking significant cultural and historical events.

general_items.name: Cracked Oracle Bones
general_items.description: These are used in a ritual involving inscribing the bones with questions to the gods then heating the bones and reading the answers from the resulting cracks. The end result are these cracked bones which imbue owner with a sense of balance and natural connection

general_items.name: Oracle Bones
general_items.description: These bones on their own have no effect but may be used in some rituals.

general_items.name: Scrying Mirror
general_items.description: A simple magical mirror used by seers to gaze into for visions and to communicate with the spiritual realm.

general_items.name: Obsidian Scrying Mirror
general_items.description: A polished piece of obsidian used by seers to gaze into for visions and to communicate with the spiritual realm.

general_items.name: Astrolabe
general_items.description: An intricate device used for solving problems relating to time and the position of the stars and planets, symbolizing the link between the celestial and terrestrial.

general_items.name: Amber Charm
general_items.description: Fossilized tree resin often carrying ancient inclusions; used for healing with its warm, soothing energy.

general_items.name: Sibylline Chalk
general_items.description: Used by the ancient Sibyls to outline prophetic texts on leaves, this chalk is said to empower words with divine foresight and clarity.

general_items.name: Celtic Knotted Rope
general_items.description: A bundle of rope magically bound in the tradition of Celtic druids delivering strength to the wielder.

general_items.name: Shamanic Corded Bindings
general_items.description: Used to bind spiritual entities or powers to the wearer. These bindings harness and direct energies necessary for healing, protection, or enhancing physical abilities.

general_items.name: Archaic Apotropaic Dime
general_items.description: This magical dime wards off evil influences

general_items.name: Rusty Bloody Nail
general_items.description: A nail with old dried blood caked into its rusted surface.

general_items.name: Vial of Abyssal Dust
general_items.description: A minuscule container holding dark, fine dust ground from a volcanic stone.

general_items.name: Deeply Treated Ash Clast
general_items.description: A small clast of ash mixed with ancient herbs, reputed to swap the meanings of nearby signs or labels for a short time.

general_items.name: Willbinding Papyrus

general_items.name: Amber Encased Cerebral Peduncle

general_items.name: Mystic Nullification Shard

general_items.description: A Small Bundle of Besom Bristles

general_items.name: Thunderstone
general_items.description: Represents one fossil

general_items.name: Fetter

general_items.name: Fragment of a Tower Shield

general_items.name: Delicate Handheld Bell
general_items.description: The harmonics of this bell resonate with the caster's intentions.

general_items.name: Goetic Dice
general_items.description: Goetic Dice are dark, rune-etched dice used to strengthen the owner's connection with dark magic.

general_items.name: Segment of Dragon's Leash
general_items.description: These segments come from previously used dragon's leashes that have been cut, enchanted, and preserved to imbue the owner with additional magical creatures skill.

general_items.name: Elderwood's Unbreakable Shield
general_items.description:
• When the owner holds this shield, they can automatically block any spell cast at them under a value of 25. 
• Spell casts over 25 risk breaking the shield permanently. Roll 1d2 (coin flip) with 1 indicating that the shield has broken and 2 indicating that the shield has remained intact.

general_items.name: Seer Stone Necklace
general_items.description: The owner can gently rub this necklace in their fingers to share any sense with a creature to with whom they have bonded. This is bidirectional meaning that the owner can share their senses with the creature or the creature can share their senses with the owner.

general_items.name: Hunter Gauntlets
general_items.description: These gloves look, at first, like any ol' quidditch glove. However, they have the ability to fire small projectiles at enemies causing 1L wound of damage (unless the item itself carries additional damage statistics) upon successful hit. The user must keep track of their ammo and specify what they are firing.

general_items.name: Command Collars
general_items.description:
The owner of this item gifts the item to a creature it has tamed or bonded with. 
• For Bonded Creatures, the owner's can send commands from any distance. This is one way communication from the owner to the bonded creature.
• For Tamed Creatures, the owner can send requests that the creature will consider. If the creature uses human social skills, have the owner and the creature roll competing social skills rolls. If they do not use social skills, have the owner roll magical creatures check with the headmaster determining the threshold for success.

general_items.name: Demon Box
general_items.description:
A Zonko's Creation, demon boxes contained a temporary conjuration of a demon that, once opened, would live for about five minutes. 

• It can cause 2L wounds of damage with its trident 
• Has only 1H wound capacity
• And has ground movement of 18

general_items.name: Chameleon Skin Gloves
general_items.description:
Chameleon Skin Gloves provide the wearer with a bonus when casting concealing spells.

Supposed to have a +2 to concealing bonus but there were bugs causing problems. So I will remove temporarily.

general_items.name: Ever-Bashing Boomerang
general_items.description: These boomerangs attack a single target. They cause 1L wound and distract the target such that any rolls take -3 penalty while attacked. The boomerang has a set value of 5 by default.

general_items.name: Magical Camera

general_items.name: Kai's Bloodsoaked Bracelet
general_items.description:
This bracelet was dowsed in the blood of its owner upon creation such that it remembers them exclusively. As such, it will only open for this owner. Breaking into the bracelet requires a reasonable strategy (as determined by the headmaster) and a roll greater than the set value of the bracelet at creation. 

The bracelet has extensively expanded carrying capacity allowing the owner to carry large items effortlessly.

general_items.name: Dermot's Weather Cube
general_items.description: This cube has the capacity to create galestorms at the whims of the owner. These storms will always exist at the set value of the cube's creation.

parts.name: Ashwinder Eggs
parts.description: Ashwinder Eggs help to ensure that a particular potion has a high potency. Usually, when it is included in a potion, the potion will be strong or enduring. Must be frozen upon discovery to save for future use.

creatures.description:
The Ashwinder was a thin pale-grey snake with glowing red eyes. They rose from the dying embers of magical fire (like floo powder) that had been allowed to burn unchecked and unsupervised. Once birthed, the Ashwinder slithered off into a dark corner to lay their eggs, leaving an ashy trail behind. Ashwinders only lived for an hour and collapsed to dust when they have laid their eggs.[1]

Ashwinder eggs were red and give off an intense heat. The heat was capable of igniting a dwelling within minutes of their birth. Wizards suspecting the presence of Ashwinders in a house had to immediate track down the nest and freeze them using an appropriate spell. Once frozen, Ashwinder eggs were very valuable; they could be used in a love potions, eaten whole as a cure for ague, or one batch could be used for the Edurus Potion.

Nogtail also cause depressing damage over time merely by being in their presence.

Fully bonding with a Thestral is impossible unless the individual as not only seen death but accepted it as well.

abilities.description: Thestrals are invisible to those that have not seen death firsthand.

abilities.name: Mount
abilities.description: This type of creature can be ridden. Flying is dependent on bond with the creature and flying skill (each serves as a bonus).

parts.name: Thestral Hide
parts.description: Not useful in potions. May be useful in either in building, as art (or as a collectible), or in artificing.

parts.name: Thestral Tail Hair
parts.description: The Thestral tail hair has powerful magic. Is used as a wand core to craft Elder Wands. Might also be useful in potions. Some say that to master this magical component, a witch or wizard must be comfortable facing death.

creatures.description: Thestrals are winged horses that are only visible to those who have seen death. To those who have unresolved feelings about the witnessed death, seeing this thestral will result in taking depressing damage. However, those who have resolved these feelings (through whatever means) will find thestrals quite docile and oddly charming.

creatures.name: Aethonan
creatures.additional_social_rules: Persuasion must rely on Magical Creatures Skill.

abilities.name: Mount
abilities.description: This type of creature can be ridden. Flying is dependent on bond with the creature and flying skill (each serves as a bonus).

parts.name: Aethonan Hide
parts.description: Not useful in potions. May be useful in either in building, as art (or as a collectible), or in artificing.

creatures.description: A breed of winged horse that is both regal and known for its agility. Aethonan become quite sedentary in the evenings and are typically, therefore, only used for work during the daylight.

creatures.name: Granian
creatures.additional_social_rules: Persuasion must rely on Magical Creatures Skill.

abilities.name: Mount
abilities.description: This type of creature can be ridden. Flying is dependent on bond with the creature and flying skill (each serves as a bonus).

parts.name: Granian Hair
parts.description: Granian Hair appears to affect one's innate magical power when added to a potion.

parts.name: Granian Hide
parts.description: Not useful in potions. May be useful in either in building, as art (or as a collectible), or in artificing.

creatures.description: A breed of winged horse that is known for its speed.

creatures.name: Hidebehind

abilities.name: Invisibility (translucent)
abilities.description: This creatures version of invisibility isn't as perfect as a true Demiguise, meaning they are still somewhat visible for those paying close attention, which is why they hide behind objects.

abilities.name: Shapeshifting
abilities.description: Their version of shapeshifting allows them to match the shape of any object they hide behind.

parts.name: Hidebehind Hide
parts.description: Not useful in potions. May be useful in either in building, as art (or as a collectible), or in artificing.

creatures.description: Hidebehinds are aggressive creatures only found in the forests of North America. These creatures hide when one attempts to view them and will attempt to sneak up on and kill their prey. Hidebehinds will attack non-human creatures, but they seem to prefer human children above other food sources. Hidebehinds find alcohol repulsive. Being part ghoul, they are resilient but weak to fire spells.

creatures.name: Glumbumble
creatures.additional_social_rules: Persuasion must rely on Magical Creatures Skill.

parts.name: Glumbumble Treacle
parts.description: Used as an antidote to the hysteria produced by eating Alihotsy leaves. Found in the hives, not on the creature itself.

creatures.description: Glumbumble are harmless magical flying insects that often infest beehives (thus ruining their honey).

creatures.name: Puffskein

parts.name: Puffskein Skin
parts.description: Not useful in potions. May be useful in either in building, as art (or as a collectible), or in artificing.

creatures.description: Puffskein are small, cuddly creatures often used as pets. While Puffstein can bite, they are typically docile.

creatures.name: Knarl
creatures.additional_social_rules: As long as you have good intention when interacting with the Knarl, it will remain friendly. If it detects any nefariousness, it will break its bonds.

parts.name: Knarl Skin
parts.description: Not useful in potions. May be useful in either in building, as art (or as a collectible), or in artificing.

parts.name: Knarl Quills
parts.description: These are similar to porcupine quills. It is known that they do not dissolve in basilisk venom. This was tested by Penelope and Alfred. Can be useful in certain potions.

creatures.description: Knarls are hedgehog-like creatures they are distrustful of humans, especially those with nefarious intent. Knarls tend to destroy gardens and other property to spite the humans living there.

creatures.name: Golden Snidget
creatures.additional_social_rules: Persuasion must rely on Magical Creatures Skill.
creatures.description: Golden Snidgets are tiny birds that used to be used as the golden snitches in Quidditch. Since their capture often result in their death, regulations were changed to allow for the charmed golden winged ball to be used instead.

creatures.name: Niffler
creatures.additional_social_rules: All persuasion attempts must rely on magical creatures skill and take a -10 penalty whenever a Niffler is attempting to acquire a shiny object.

abilities.name: Treasure Hunter
abilities.description: Skilled at finding nearby treasure in any location. Some wizards and witches have even trained them to use this ability to seek out a wide variety of valuable objects, not just shiny objects.

abilities.name: Skilled Thief
abilities.description: Nifflers can into hard to enter places in search of treasure. They can even burrow underground for treasure or maneuver through cursed sites with relative ease. (Passive ability that allows Nifflers to enter places most people can't)

abilities.name: Deep Pockets
abilities.description: Nifflers have a pouch that works similar to the Extension charm, capable enough objects to fill a large room.

abilities.name: Squeeze
abilities.description: Can squeeze into tight cracks bypassing locks and traps.

parts.name: Niffler Skin
parts.description: Not useful in potions. May be useful in either in building, as art (or as a collectible), or in artificing.

creatures.description: Niffler are attracted to shiny objects and are particularly mischievious in their efforts to obtain these objects. Their drive for finding and hoarding these objects is nearly insatiable. Nifflers will eventually turn their space into a wreck as the pile of objects they collect grows bigger and bigger, which can be a problem for any owner of a Niffler. When not hunting for treasure, Niffler are typically found asleep or eating small grubs.

creatures.name: Moke
creatures.additional_social_rules: If initial attempt to tame fails then this creature will shrink to the point of no longer being visible, and thus, no further persuasion attempts will be possible.

abilities.name: Shrinkage
abilities.description: This creature can shrink to the point of no longer being visible to the human eye.

parts.name: Moke Skin
parts.description: Not useful in potions. Useful to craft bags or items that shrink at will. Makes theft impossible as the bag and all contents inside can shrink until they are no longer visible.

creatures.description: Moke are lizard-like creatures capable of shrinking at will to hide from predators and can become small enough as to be undetectable to human eyes. Bags made from the skin are very valuable, as the bags will shrink whenever thieves attempt to steal them.

creatures.name: Kneazle
creatures.additional_social_rules: Persuasion must be based on social skill and will take a penalty of 15.

abilities.name: Judgement
abilities.description: This creature can detect suspicious or untrustworthy individuals, and will act standoffish with such individuals. This will also affect persuasion attempts.

parts.name: Kneazel Hair
parts.description: Kneazle hair is an often essential ingredient to creating complex magical creatures-linked potions.

parts.name: Kneazle Skin
parts.description: Not useful in potions. May be useful in either in building, as art (or as a collectible), or in artificing.

parts.name: Kneazle Whisker
parts.description: Used as a wand core.

creatures.description: Kneazles are highly intelligent cat-like creatures. They have a very small amount of human intelligence and social skill which they can use to understand complex commands and act on behalf of their owner without instruction.

creatures.name: Diricawl

abilities.name: Vanish
abilities.description: Can disappear in a flash without being detectably traced.

parts.name: Diricawl Skin
parts.description: Not useful in potions. May be useful in either in building, as art (or as a collectible), or in artificing.

creatures.description: Diricawl (also known as Dodo birds) are believed by muggles to be extinct. However, they are actually able to apparate short distances to evade danger. Approaching a diricawl is quite difficult and requires repeated rolls of 20 to avoid spooking the creature. If spooked, the Diricawl will immediately apparate. However, if handled appropriately, Diricawl will allow humans to handle them. Diricawl are flightless.

creatures.name: Demiguise

abilities.name: Precognitive sight
abilities.description: This creature can predict the immediate future of anything it sees. This ability shows it the most probable events, which means the more spontaneous or random the event, the less likely it can predict it.

abilities.name: Invisibility
abilities.description: This creature can turn invisible.

parts.name: Demiguise Skin
parts.description: Demiguise pelts were highly sought after as the hair can be woven into Invisibility Cloaks. These cloaks become more visible over time.

parts.name: Demiguise Blood
parts.description: Can be used in potions for invisibility or increased perception.

creatures.description: Demiguise resemble sloths, have the power of invisibility, and can predict the future. Because of this, they are very difficult to catch. A Demiguise must be convinced completely that you mean it no harm before it will allow for another to come near it. Their hair is extremely valuable, as it can be used to create invisibility cloaks (however, these cloaks lose transparency over time). Typically found in Japan, China and Korea.

creatures.name: Dugbog
creatures.additional_social_rules: Persuasion must be based on social skill and will take a penalty of 15.

parts.name: Dugbog Skin
parts.description: Not useful in potions. May be useful in either in building, as art (or as a collectible), or in artificing.

parts.name: Dugbog Tongue
parts.description: Useful in potions.

creatures.description: Dugbogs are aggressive, small, marshland creatures that resemble pieces of dead bark (usually floating in water). When not biting you, dugbogs mostly just float. They can swim very slowly and inefficiently but mostly choose not to bother.

creatures.name: Hinkypunk
creatures.additional_social_rules: Hinkypunks cannot be persuaded by humans. If you attempt to do so, they will be able to freely attempt to rapture you even when it is not their turn.

attacks.name: Lure
attacks.description: When successful, the Hinkypunk lures the target to follow it deeper into the forest. The target may attempt to break free using a mental fortitude roll. If lured, the target has no control over their movements or actions until they break free.

creatures.description: Hinkypunks are spirits that appear to walk on one leg. They are found in the forests in Scotland (especially) but may be seen in other areas of the British Isles and Western Europe. Hinkypunks will attempt to coerce you into following them. While this does not damage you, you may be lured into danger if raptured by them. You will not have control of your body if lured by a Hinkypunk. However, you may attempt to break free of the enchantment by making a willpower roll (set value 21). Hinkypunks are non-corporeal so no physically damaging spells will work on it.

creatures.name: Red Cap
creatures.additional_social_rules: Red caps are mostly interested in human bloodshed, so any efforts to persuade must involve promise of blood to feast upon. If a red cap is already contented with their store of blood, they will not be persuadable. However, if they are able in need of blood, they can be persuaded. Persuasion is based on Social Skills rather than Magical Creatures skill.
creatures.description: Red caps are goblin-like creatures that inhabit land which has previously shed human blood. Red caps are capable of feeding off of the blood for decades without needing any additional sustinence thereby making moot the need for migration. Red Caps are able to cast level 1 spells from the Goblin spellbook using human femurs as if they were wands.

creatures.name: Jackalope
creatures.additional_social_rules: Persuasion must be based on Magical Creatures skill.

parts.name: Jackalope Skin
parts.description: Not useful in potions. May be useful in either in building, as art (or as a collectible), or in artificing.

creatures.description: Jackalopes are aggressive jackrabbit-like creatures with antelope horns. They are typically found in North America.

creatures.name: Snallygaster
creatures.additional_social_rules: Snallygaster can be persuaded with a Magical Creatures roll of 15.

parts.name: Snallygaster Scales
parts.description: Not useful in potions. May be useful in either in building, as art (or as a collectible), or in artificing.

creatures.description: Snallygaster are part bird, part reptile creatures typically found in the area around Maryland in America.

creatures.name: Horned Serpent
creatures.additional_social_rules: Horned Serpents can only be persuaded using parsletongue and Magical Creatures skill combined.

parts.name: Horned Serpent Skin
parts.description: Not useful in potions. May be useful in either in building, as art (or as a collectible), or in artificing.

creatures.description: Horned Serpents are violent serpents typically found in bodies of fresh water in North America. These serpents are believed to be more intelligent than typical serpents. Their squeeze attack can only be used on small (size 1) creatures.

creatures.name: Rougarou
creatures.additional_social_rules: Rougarou cannot be tamed, but may be intimidated (though this is quite difficult).

parts.name: Rougarou Hide
parts.description: Not useful in potions. May be useful in either in building, as art (or as a collectible), or in artificing.

creatures.description: Also known as the Louisiana Werewolf, Rouxgarou rugaroo or rugaru. Rougarou look similar to a werewolf in wolf form but are, in fact, permanently in beast form (and were never human at all). They are commonly found in Louisiana prowling swamps. Their bite/tear attack causes 1M wound every minute from bleeding out.

creatures.name: White River Monster
creatures.additional_social_rules: Cannot be persuaded by humans.
creatures.description: The White River Monster is a monsterous fish found in the White River in Arkansas. It is recognizable by the spines on its back.

creatures.name: Wampus Cat
creatures.additional_social_rules: Persuasion is based on Magical Creatures skill and the creature will attack if the first persuasion attempt is unsuccessful.

parts.name: Wampus Cat Skin
parts.description: Not useful in potions. May be useful in either in building, as art (or as a collectible), or in artificing.

creatures.description: The Wampus Cat is a cat-like magical beast with six legs.

creatures.name: Thunderbird
creatures.additional_social_rules: Thunderbirds can be persuaded using Magical Creatures skill (15).

parts.name: Thunderbird Skin
parts.description: Not useful in potions. May be useful in either in building, as art (or as a collectible), or in artificing.

parts.name: Thunderbird's Tail Feather
parts.description: These tail feathers have high magical properties, and can be used as wand cores to craft very rare yet powerful wands. Such wands provide a high boost to transfiguration spells and have the same ability as the Thunderbird to sense danger, and automatically cast counter curses passively.

creatures.description: Thunderbirds are large, violent birds that create lightning storms as they attack. They are considered highly dangerous. Typically found in Arizona. Its lightning attack will affect all creatures in the area.

creatures.name: Streeler
creatures.additional_social_rules: Streelers are far too dumb to be persuaded or really have any high level motivation whatsoever.

parts.name: Streeler Skin
parts.description: Not useful in potions. May be useful in either in building, as art (or as a collectible), or in artificing.

parts.name: Streeler Shells
parts.description: Streeler Shells are useful in creature-repelling spells. They may also be useful in herbicides and fungicides.

parts.name: Streeler Slime
parts.description: The slime is so toxic that it burned all vegetation that it touches. Fatal to Horklumps.

creatures.description: Streeler are magical snails with poisonous slime.

creatures.name: Giant Purple Toad
creatures.additional_social_rules: Giant Purple Toads can be persuaded to come when called but cannot understand more complex commands.

parts.name: Giant Purple Toad Skin
parts.description: Not useful in potions. May be useful in either in building, as art (or as a collectible), or in artificing.

parts.name: Giant Purple Toad Warts
parts.description: Giant Purple Toad Warts appear to affect either intelligence itself or intelligence-linked skills.

creatures.description: Giant Purple Toads are mostly harmless creatures, but their croaks are known to petrify any who hear them in close proximity. Luckily this petrification wears off naturally after about 1 minute, but because of this, many institutions have banned them and their numbers have begun to be

creatures.name: Flitterby Moth
creatures.additional_social_rules: Flitterby moths cannot be persuaded. They are attracted to heat.

parts.name: Crushed Flitterby Moth
parts.description: Crushed Flitterby Moths can be an effective and powerful fertilizing agent as well as an endurance-producing agent.

creatures.description: Flitterby moths are small and golden colored. They are considered harmless and can often be heard making a faint laughing sound.

creatures.name: Flying Seahorse
creatures.additional_social_rules: Persuasion must rely on Magical Creatures skill.
creatures.description: Flying seahorses are harlmess creatures that resemble seahorses but can fly.

creatures.name: Boomslang
creatures.additional_social_rules: Persuasion must rely on use of Parsletongue + Magical Creatures skill.

parts.name: Boomslang Skin
parts.description: Boomslang Skin can be used to help transfigure a potion's consumer.

creatures.description: Boomslang are large venomous snakes found typically in Africa.

creatures.name: Bicorn
creatures.additional_social_rules: Persuasion must rely on Magical Creatures skill.

parts.name: Bicorn Hide
parts.description: Not useful in potions. May be useful in either in building, as art (or as a collectible), or in artificing.

parts.name: Bicorn Hair
parts.description: Bicorn Hair, when added to a potion, will produce a physical/bodily transformation of some kind.

creatures.description: Bicorn are violent double-horned cow-like creatures. Their horns are shed anually and they are known to be most vulnerable as they are regrowing them. Their attacks both cost vital damage over time.

creatures.name: Lacewing Fly
creatures.additional_social_rules: Lacewing flies cannot be persuaded, but could be lured by the right kinds of food options.

parts.name: Collection of Lacewing Flies
parts.description: Lacewing Flies are used in potions that affect something with respect to the body.

creatures.description: Lacewing flis are harmless green flyinng insects with transparent wings.

creatures.name: Shrake
creatures.additional_social_rules: Cannot be persuaded by humans.

parts.name: Shrake Skin
parts.description: Not useful in potions. May be useful in either in building, as art (or as a collectible), or in artificing.

parts.name: Shrake Spines
parts.description: Shrake spines are believed to be potentially dangerous in potions, potentially causing the drinker of such a potion to suffer multiple lacerations. However, the exact formula for this potion has been lost to history. Alternatively, it seems to be useful in the Boil-Aid recipe. So that's unexpected!?

creatures.description: Shrakes are magical fish with spines covering their bodies. They are aggressive and known to destroy fishing nets. They are most often found in the Atlantic Ocean.

creatures.name: Horned Slug
creatures.additional_social_rules: Horned slugs cannot be persuaded but can be lured with certain foods.
creatures.description: Horned slugs are harmless slugs with horns on their heads.

creatures.name: Horklump
creatures.additional_social_rules: Horned slugs cannot be persuaded but can be lured with certain foods.

parts.name: Horklump Skin
parts.description: Not useful in potions. May be useful in either in building, as art (or as a collectible), or in artificing.

creatures.description: Horklumps are mushroom-lke creatures which have bristles covering them. They appear to be mushrooms, but are actually animals.

creatures.name: Unicorn
creatures.additional_social_rules: Unicorns can be persuaded using Magical Creatures skill. Wizards take a -5 penalty when trying to tame an adult unicorn, since they tend to favor women.

attacks.name: Harpoon
attacks.description: Strikes with the large horn on its head.

parts.name: Unicorn Blood
parts.description: Unicorn blood alone is a powerful healing agent. Drinking it directly can stave off any damage over time and prevent the drinker from dying. It automatically heals 7H wounds with every drink. However, the drinker will suffer a permanent darkening of their soul.

parts.name: Unicorn Hair
parts.description: Unicorn is an stabilizing agent in healing potions or in beautifying products.

parts.name: Unicorn Hide
parts.description: Not useful in potions. May be useful in either in building, as art (or as a collectible), or in artificing.

parts.name: Whole Unicorn Horn
parts.description: Many potioneers believe that these horns have incredible power that can be used in potioncrafting. However, no widely known uses are yet available.

parts.name: Unicorn Tail Hair
parts.description: Used as a wand core and has strong magical properties.

creatures.description: Unicorns are magestic beasts with a single horn protruding from the center of its forehead. Unicorns will only fight in self-defense. Almost every part of the Unicorn can be used in healing potions due to the purifying properties; however, those who drink pure unicorn blood are said to live a cursed existence in exchange for being healed from any injury, regardless of how close to death they are.

creatures.name: Qilin
creatures.additional_social_rules: Qilin can be persuaded using Magical Creatures skill rolls of 25 or better.

parts.name: Qilin Skin
parts.description: Not useful in potions. May be useful in either in building, as art (or as a collectible), or in artificing.

creatures.description: Qilin are East-Asian beasts that have a single horn coming out of the middle of their forehead. Because of this single horn, Qilin are more violent and less magical than traditional unicorns.

creatures.name: Mooncalf
creatures.additional_social_rules: Persuasion must rely on Magical Creatures skill.

parts.name: Mooncalf Dung
parts.description: Mooncalf dung appears to be particularly helpful in supporting plant growth and health.

parts.name: Mooncalf Skin
parts.description: Not useful in potions. May be useful in either in building, as art (or as a collectible), or in artificing.

creatures.description: Mooncalves are timid magical beasts that only come out during a full moon. They are adorable and docile creatures.

creatures.name: Siren
creatures.additional_social_rules: Sirens can be persuaded by humans. The siren rolls a human social skills roll. The persuader needs to better the siren human social skills roll and must also resist the sirens song. If both are achieved, the siren is successfully persuaded.

parts.name: Siren Skin
parts.description: Not useful in potions. May be useful in either in building, as art (or as a collectible), or in artificing.

creatures.description: Sirens are a race of merperson known for their alluring beauty. While the Siren song will not damage the individual, the individual will be drawn to the Siren and will not be able to pursue other goals until it breaks free of the song (using a magical creatures roll greater than the associated value).

creatures.name: Nundu
creatures.additional_social_rules: Persuasion must be based on Magical Creatures. Flying is dependent on bond with the creature and flying skill (each serves as a bonus). It is believed that a natural 10 and a combined score of 50 would be required to successfully mount one of these.

parts.name: Nundu Claw
parts.description: Nundu claw is traditionally only known to work in a single potion, the Muffling Draught. While others may have found other uses for it, few potioneers believe that this dangerous-to-retreive item is worth the effort. Because of its scarcity, this item is highly valued.

parts.name: Nundu Skin
parts.description: Not useful in potions. May be useful in either in building, as art (or as a collectible), or in artificing.

creatures.description: Nundu are spikey large cats with toxic breath. These powerful cats are among the most dangerous beasts known to wizard-kind.

creatures.name: Werewolf
creatures.additional_social_rules: Werewolved cannot be tamed, but may be intimidated (though this is quite difficult).

parts.name: Werewolf Hide
parts.description: Not useful in potions. May be useful in either in building, as art (or as a collectible), or in artificing.

creatures.description: Werewolves are simply a transformed, but dangerous, human.

creatures.name: Chimera
creatures.additional_social_rules: Chimera cannot be persuaded.

parts.name: Chimera Hide
parts.description: Not useful in potions. May be useful in either in building, as art (or as a collectible), or in artificing.

creatures.description: Chimera are hybrid beasts with the head of a lion, great wings, and the tail of a serpent. They are among the most dangerous creatures known to Wizard-kind.

creatures.name: Basilisk
creatures.additional_social_rules: Basilisks can be persuaded with an exceptional magical creatures roll and parsletongue.

parts.name: Basilisk Skin
parts.description: Not useful in potions. May be useful in either in building, as art (or as a collectible), or in artificing.

creatures.description: Basilisks are massive dangerous serpents with the ability to kill with direct eye contact (and petrify with indirect eye contact; use mortal gaze stats without assigning damage). If petrified, the individual is immobile and unable to take actions or take turns until they are successfully un-petrified). They are known as among the most dangerous creatures known to wizard-kind.

creatures.name: Cerberus
creatures.additional_social_rules: Cerberus can be persuaded with a magical creatures.

parts.name: Cerberus Skin
parts.description: Not useful in potions. May be useful in either in building, as art (or as a collectible), or in artificing.

creatures.description: Cerberus are multi-headed dogs that can vary widely in size and danger. Add one more heavy wound for each attack for each size over 2. For example, if the Cerberus is size 5, take the bite wound (which is 1M at baseline) and add 3 (because 5-2=3). Thus the bite attack would do 3H wounds. Round down to whole heavy wounds (i.e., size 5 = 3H rather than 3H+1M).

creatures.name: Hydra
creatures.additional_social_rules: Cerberus can be persuaded with a magical creatures.

parts.name: Hydra Skin
parts.description: Not useful in potions. May be useful in either in building, as art (or as a collectible), or in artificing.

creatures.description: Hydra are massive water-dwelling multi-headed dangerous beasts. Roll 1d4+1 to determine the number of heads the Hydra has. Each stat applies to one individual head and each one must be killed individually (or the beasts body must be killled. Each head gets its own turn. If the Hydra gasps an individual, they will pull them under the water causing crushing damage and vital damage (due to drowning).

creatures.name: Hungarian Horntail
creatures.additional_social_rules: Dragons can be persuaded (Magical Creatures roll of 30), Intimidated (Magical Creatures roll of 25 along with dealing at least 2 heavy wounds of damage), or Lured (an appropriate lure placed in sight without agitating the dragon.

parts.name: Dragon Claw
parts.description: Dragon claw isn't known to have a general use, though it's possible that one exists. However, it is an essential ingredient in Dragon-Gash Healing Paste.

parts.name: Dragon Dung
parts.description: Dragon dung is incredibly useful as a fertilizer and is essential for growing any plant with a skill level of higher than 5.

parts.name: Dragon Liver
parts.description: Dragon Livers are thought to be useful in digestion potions and are believed to be effective in creating tamper-resistant solutions or magically resistant potions.

parts.name: Dragon's Blood
parts.description: Blood from a dragon.

parts.name: Dragon's Tongue
parts.description: There is no known use for a Dragon's Tongue in a potion. Some scholars believe that the only reason potioncrafter's keep these is so that they can say that they have one.

parts.name: Hungarian Horntail Hide
parts.description: Not useful in potions. May be useful in either in building, as art (or as a collectible), or in artificing.

creatures.description: Hungarian Horntails are a dangerous breed of dragon. They are identifiable due to their black spines and horn-spiked club,

creatures.name: Ukrainian Ironbelly
creatures.additional_social_rules: Dragons can be persuaded (Magical Creatures roll of 30), Intimidated (Magical Creatures roll of 25 along with dealing at least 2 heavy wounds of damage), or Lured (an appropriate lure placed in sight without agitating the dragon.

parts.name: Dragon Claw
parts.description: Dragon claw isn't known to have a general use, though it's possible that one exists. However, it is an essential ingredient in Dragon-Gash Healing Paste.

parts.name: Dragon Dung
parts.description: Dragon dung is incredibly useful as a fertilizer and is essential for growing any plant with a skill level of higher than 5.

parts.name: Dragon Liver
parts.description: Dragon Livers are thought to be useful in digestion potions and are believed to be effective in creating tamper-resistant solutions or magically resistant potions.

parts.name: Dragon's Blood
parts.description: Blood from a dragon.

parts.name: Dragon's Tongue
parts.description: There is no known use for a Dragon's Tongue in a potion. Some scholars believe that the only reason potioncrafter's keep these is so that they can say that they have one.

parts.name: Ukrainian Ironbelly Hide
parts.description: Not useful in potions. May be useful in either in building, as art (or as a collectible), or in artificing.

creatures.description: Ukrainian Ironbellies are a dangerous breed of dragon. They are suspected to be the largest of the dragon breeds on earth.

creatures.name: Norwegian Ridgeback
creatures.additional_social_rules: Norwegian Ridgebacks can be persuaded (Magical Creatures roll of 22), Intimidated (Magical Creatures roll of 20 along with dealing at least 1 heavy wound of damage), or Lured (an appropriate lure placed in sight without agitating the dragon).

parts.name: Dragon Claw
parts.description: Dragon claw isn't known to have a general use, though it's possible that one exists. However, it is an essential ingredient in Dragon-Gash Healing Paste.

parts.name: Dragon Dung
parts.description: Dragon dung is incredibly useful as a fertilizer and is essential for growing any plant with a skill level of higher than 5.

parts.name: Dragon Liver
parts.description: Dragon Livers are thought to be useful in digestion potions and are believed to be effective in creating tamper-resistant solutions or magically resistant potions.

parts.name: Dragon's Tongue
parts.description: There is no known use for a Dragon's Tongue in a potion. Some scholars believe that the only reason potioncrafter's keep these is so that they can say that they have one.

parts.name: Norwegian Ridgeback Hide
parts.description: Not useful in potions. May be useful in either in building, as art (or as a collectible), or in artificing.

creatures.description: Norwegian Ridgebacks are a dangerous breed of dragon. Norwegian Ridgebacks are smaller and less hostile, but have venomous fangs. If the Norwegian Ridgeback is size 5, its damage amounts are all +1 damage amount.

They can also be smaller than many other dragons, and their firebreath are seen as balls of fire rather than continuous streams like most other dragons. Chinese Fireballs are more tolerant of other dragons and may be willing to share territory with them. Lastly, they are known to be much faster than other dragons.

creatures.name: Peruvian Vipertooth
creatures.additional_social_rules: Peruvian Vipertooths cannot be persuaded at they will immediately attempt to eat any human they see. They can be intimidated (Magical Creatures roll of 20 along with dealing at least 1 heavy wound of damage), or Lured (an appropriate lure placed in sight without agitating the dragon).

parts.description: Not useful in potions. May be useful in either in building, as art (or as a collectible), or in artificing.

parts.name: Peruvian Vipertooth Venom
parts.description: A powerful venom that can cause significant damage even to very large creatures.

creatures.description: Peruvian Vipertooth are a dangerous breed of dragon. They are identifiable by their copper color and are typically found exclusively in Peru. Most are still infected with a variant of the Dragon Pox. Anyone who touches a Vipertooth will have a chance of being infected and cannot roll a dodge or other type of evasion. Gloves and other protective gear are also not known to work. Every time one is touched, the risk of infection is present.

creatures.description: Swedish Short Snouts are a dangerous breed of dragon. They are identifiable (obviously) by the short snouts, their blueish silver skin, and their blue flames. Their flame is known as the hottest known to wizard-kind. Short Snouts rarely come into contact with humans but are aggressive when they do.

creatures.additional_social_rules: Swedish Short Snouts can be persuaded (Magical Creatures roll of 26), Intimidated (Magical Creatures roll of 21 along with dealing at least 1 heavy wounds of damage), or Lured (an appropriate lure placed in sight without agitating the dragon).

creatures.description: Romanian Longhorns are a dangerous breed of dragon. They are identifiable (obviously) by their green skin and golden horns. It prefers to gore than to use its far less effective and weak fire breath. If the longhorn is size 5, all damages are +2.

creatures.additional_social_rules: Antipodean Opaleyes can be persuaded (Magical Creatures roll of 20), Intimidated (Magical Creatures roll of 13 along with dealing at least 1 heavy wound of damage), or Lured (an appropriate lure placed in sight without agitating the dragon).

parts.description: Not useful in potions. May be useful in either in building, as art (or as a collectible), or in artificing.

creatures.description: Antipodean Opaleyes are a relatively docile and beautiful breed of dragon. They tend not to attack unless provoked or hungry. They are considered the most majestic and beautiful dragons on earth and tend only to feed on sheep, goats, and kangaroos. They have rarely been seen outside of Australia. They are found more in valleys than in mountains (a rare trait among dragons).

creatures.name: Hebridean Black Dragon
creatures.additional_social_rules: Hebridean Black Dragon can be persuaded (Magical Creatures roll of 30), Intimidated (Magical Creatures roll of 25 along with dealing at least 3 heavy wound of damage), or Lured (an appropriate lure placed in sight without agitating the dragon).

parts.name: Hebridean Black Dragon Hide
parts.description: Not useful in potions. May be useful in either in building, as art (or as a collectible), or in artificing.

creatures.description: Hebridean Black Dragon are dangerous dragons identifiable by their purple eyes. These dragons have ridges on their back and a sharp spiked tail. They are highly aggressive but have lower magical resistance.

creatures.name: Common Welsh Green Dragon
creatures.additional_social_rules: Welsh Greens prefer to avoid humans and will try to get away as their first action. They can be persuaded (Magical Creatures roll of 18), Intimidated (Magical Creatures roll of 12 along with dealing at least 3 heavy wound of damage), or Lured (an appropriate lure placed in sight without agitating the dragon).

parts.name: Welsh Green Dragon Hide
parts.description: Not useful in potions. May be useful in either in building, as art (or as a collectible), or in artificing.

parts.name: Dragon Claw
parts.description: Dragon claw isn't known to have a general use, though it's possible that one exists. However, it is an essential ingredient in Dragon-Gash Healing Paste.

parts.name: Dragon's Tongue
parts.description: There is no known use for a Dragon's Tongue in a potion. Some scholars believe that the only reason potioncrafter's keep these is so that they can say that they have one.

creatures.description: Common Welsh Green Dragons are a smaller, but still aggressive, dragons. They are green in appearance and have a melodic roar.

creatures.name: Common Giant
creatures.additional_social_rules: Giants can be persuaded through provision of gifts and a Magical Creatures roll of 15 and a Social Skill roll of 2. They can be intimidated by dealing 3 heavy wounds and a Social Skills roll of 2.

parts.name: Giant Skin
parts.description: Not useful in potions. May be useful in either in building, as art (or as a collectible), or in artificing.

creatures.description: Giants are oversized human-like creatures known for being aggressive and dumb.

creatures.name: Oversized Giant
creatures.additional_social_rules: Oversized Giants can be persuaded through provision of gifts and a Magical Creatures roll of 20 and a social skill roll of 2. They can be intimidated by dealing 4 heavy wounds and a Social Skills roll of 2.

parts.name: Giant Skin
parts.description: Not useful in potions. May be useful in either in building, as art (or as a collectible), or in artificing.

creatures.description: Oversized Giants are larger-than-typical Giants known for being even more aggressive and even dumber.

creatures.name: Giant Runt
creatures.additional_social_rules: Runts can be persuaded through provision of gifts and a Magical Creatures roll of 10 and a Social Skill roll of 2. They can be intimidated by dealing 1 heavy wound and a Social Skills roll of 2.

parts.name: Giant Skin
parts.description: Not useful in potions. May be useful in either in building, as art (or as a collectible), or in artificing.

creatures.description: Giant runts are smaller giants. If the runt is size 4, all damage is -1

creatures.name: Gurg
creatures.additional_social_rules: Gurgs can be persuaded through provision of gifts and a Magical Creatures roll of 25 and a social skill roll of 2. They can be intimidated by dealing 5 heavy wounds and a Social Skills roll of 2. If persuaded or intimidated, the Gurg and their clan will be under the command of the attacker.
creatures.description: Gurgs are oversized giant leaders. They are the most aggressive, typically.

creatures.name: Quintaped
creatures.additional_social_rules: Quintaped cannot be tamed or persuaded by humans.

parts.name: Quintaped Skin
parts.description: Not useful in potions. May be useful in either in building, as art (or as a collectible), or in artificing.

creatures.description: Quintaped are dangerous five-legged beasts. They have a particular loathing for humans and are always violent toward them.

creatures.name: Griffin
creatures.additional_social_rules: Griffins can be intimidated. They cannot be persuaded. They can be bonded with but any attempts to bond teach it to perform any trick or task comes with a risk of being attacked. Any rolls under 3 (on the dice) will result in being attacked.

parts.name: Griffin Hide
parts.description: Not useful in potions. May be useful in either in building, as art (or as a collectible), or in artificing.

parts.name: Powdered Griffin Claw
parts.description: Powdered Griffin Claw is essential for some very powerful potions. While its effects are diverse, it can be used as the primary ingredient in a complex potion.

creatures.description: Griffins are a hybrid species with an eagle head and the body and legs of a lion. They are quite aggressive and difficult to bond with.

creatures.name: Centaur
creatures.additional_social_rules: Centaurs mainly interact with humans using regular human skills (intelligence and social skills).

parts.name: Centaur Blood
parts.description: Poisonous when touching the skin. Often added to shirts or chitons to slowly poison the wearer until dead.

parts.name: Centaur Hide
parts.description: Not useful in potions. May be useful in either in building, as art (or as a collectible), or in artificing.

creatures.description: Centaurs are hybrid creatures with a human torso and head and the body of a horse. They are particularly adept at astronomy and archery.

creatures.name: Sea Serpent
creatures.additional_social_rules: Sea Serpents can be persuaded using Parsletongue.

parts.name: Sea Serpent Scales
parts.description: Not useful in potions. May be useful in either in building, as art (or as a collectible), or in artificing.

creatures.description: Sea Serpents are massive and dangerous water-dwelling beasts with a horse-like head and serpent body. They are rare but among the most dangerous creatures known to wizard-kind but are typically non-aggressive (unless attacked).

creatures.name: Selma
creatures.additional_social_rules: Selma can be persuaded using parsletongue.

parts.name: Selma Scales
parts.description: Not useful in potions. May be useful in either in building, as art (or as a collectible), or in artificing.

creatures.description: Selma are massive and dangerous water-dwelling beasts with a horse-like head and serpent body. They are rare but among the most dangerous creatures known to wizard-kind.

creatures.name: Cockatrice
creatures.additional_social_rules: Cockatrice can be persuaded using magical creatures or through bonding.

parts.name: Cockatrice Feather
parts.description: Cockatrice feather can produce a paralytic effect in potions. However its use in the anti-paralysis potion is believed to be produced as a result of another component that has a reversing effect.

parts.name: Cockatrice Skin
parts.description: Not useful in potions. May be useful in either in building, as art (or as a collectible), or in artificing.

creatures.description: Cockatrice are dangerous rooster-like creatures with the tail of a lizard. Their peck has a chance to petrify its victim (when a peck is successful both roll 1d4, if the cockatrice rolls higher, the victim is petrified).

creatures.name: Harpy
creatures.additional_social_rules: Harpy can be persuaded using magical creatures or through bonding.

parts.name: Harpy Skin
parts.description: Not useful in potions. May be useful in either in building, as art (or as a collectible), or in artificing.

creatures.description: Harpy are dangerous bird-like hybrid creatures with the a human female head and the body of a bird. Despite having a human head, they possess very little human intelligence and no human speech. They are considered non-sentient.

creatures.name: Yeti
creatures.additional_social_rules: Trolls cannot be persuaded by humans. They can be intimidated and lured.

parts.name: Yeti Skin
parts.description: Not useful in potions. May be useful in either in building, as art (or as a collectible), or in artificing.

creatures.description: Yeti are massive two-legged beasts they almost exclusively live in cold weather areas.

creatures.name: Tebo
creatures.additional_social_rules: Persuasion must be based on Magical Creatures.

parts.name: Tebo Skin
parts.description: Not useful in potions. May be useful in either in building, as art (or as a collectible), or in artificing.

parts.name: Tebo Tusk
parts.description: Tebo Tusk is essential to invisibility-linked potions or to any potions that appear to affect the appearance or visibility of the drinker.

creatures.description: Tebo are warthog like-creatures typically found in Africa. They are able to make themselves invisible.

creatures.name: Runespoor
creatures.additional_social_rules: Horned Serpents can only be persuaded using parsletongue and Magical Creatures skill combined.

parts.name: Runespoor Fangs
parts.description: Runespoor Fangs are typically drained of their venom before use in potions. For an unknown reason, the fangs themselves appear to impact intelligence or intelligence-linked skills.

parts.name: Runespoor Skin
parts.description: Not useful in potions. May be useful in either in building, as art (or as a collectible), or in artificing.

creatures.description: Runespoor are dangerous three-headed African snakes. They typically live in unplottable forests near Burkina Faso. Each head gets its own attack. All damage is increased by two wounds if the creature size is 3.

creatures.name: Re’em
creatures.additional_social_rules: Cannot be tamed or persuaded by humans.

parts.name: Re'em Hide
parts.description: Not useful in potions. May be useful in either in building, as art (or as a collectible), or in artificing.

parts.name: Re'em Blood
parts.description: Re'Em Blood is linked to magical power in potions. According to lore, it can provide a substantial physical strength buff. Essentially giving the drinker super strength for a limited time.

creatures.description: Re’em are rare ox-like creatures that have been hunted nearly to extinction because of the value of their blood.

creatures.name: Occamy
creatures.additional_social_rules: Occamy cannot be persuaded. They can be lured.

parts.name: Occamy Eggshell
parts.description: The exact function of occamy eggshells is unknown. However, they appear to be associated with increasing the potency of spells. Many potioneers find that adding Occamy Eggshell to an otherwise failing potion can give it the boost it needs to finally work!

parts.name: Occamy Skin
parts.description: Not useful in potions. May be useful in either in building, as art (or as a collectible), or in artificing.

creatures.description: Occamy are beautiful and aggressive winged serpents that grow or shrink to fit the space they presently occupy. They are feircely protective of their eggs. Its bite damage depends on its size. Size 1 = 1L, Size 2 = 1M, Size 3 = 1H, Size 4 = 3H, and Size 5 = 5H. The associated value always remains the same.

creatures.name: Kappa
creatures.additional_social_rules: Cannot be persuaded by humans.

parts.name: Kappa Cap
parts.description: Not useful in potions. May be useful in either in building, as art (or as a collectible), or in artificing.

parts.name: Kappa Skin
parts.description: Not useful in potions. May be useful in either in building, as art (or as a collectible), or in artificing.

creatures.description: Kappa are dangerous water-demons mostly found in Asia. They dwell in shallow water and strangle any creatures that come near. +5 to the associated value.

creatures.name: Kelpie (Default)
creatures.additional_social_rules: Cannot be persuaded by humans. Can be rendered docile.

parts.name: Humanform Kelpie Hair
parts.description: Can be used as a wand core. Might have other magical properties that can be explored.

parts.name: Horseform Kelpie Hair
parts.description: Can be used as a wand core. Might have other magical properties that can be explored.

parts.name: Horseform Kelpie Hide
parts.description: Not useful in potions. May be useful in either in building, as art (or as a collectible), or in artificing.

creatures.description: Kelpie are shape shifters, able to take any form they know about. They tend to take the form of a horse (all statistics listed with this entry are for the Kelpie's Horse Form). If they transform, they take on the statistics of the creature into which they have transformed (all damage sustained is transferred). A common myth suggests that they can be tamed through use of a bridle when in horse form. In reality, they can be tamed in whatever form so long as the tamer uses the correct methods for that beast. Kelpie will often encourage humans to ride their backs then carry their prey into the water and attempt to eat them.

creatures.name: Graphorn
creatures.additional_social_rules: Graphorns can be persuaded using magical creatures skill (roll >20).

parts.name: Graphorn Hide
parts.description: Not useful in potions. Resistant to most spells and extremely tough.

parts.name: Graphorn Horns
parts.description: Two golden horns. Might be useful in healing potions.

creatures.description: Graphorns are four-legged, hump-backed creatures that walk on four legs. They are extremely magically resistant and very dangerous.

creatures.name: Erumpent
creatures.additional_social_rules: Erumpents can be persuaded using magical creatures skill (roll >15).

parts.name: Erumpent Hide
parts.description: Not useful in potions. However, the hide has a magic resistance that repels most charms and curses.

parts.name: Erumpent Horn
parts.description: No widely known potion involves the use of an entire, whole Erumpent horn. However, its chark and fluid can be separated and are individually quite valuable. It requires a Magical Creatures roll of 15 to separate these. Failure to do so results in an explosion which can cause between 6 and 8 heavy wounds instantly.

parts.name: Erumpent Tail
parts.description: Erumpent tail is a minor reagent that appears essential to creating an Erumption Potion, but has not been found to have any use in other potions.

creatures.description: Erumpents are rhinoceros-like beasts with a single glowing horn. The horn itself contains a dangerously explosive fluid. When gouged with the horn, the horn fluid will explode.

creatures.name: Troll
creatures.additional_social_rules: Trolls cannot be persuaded by humans. They can be intimidated and lured.

parts.name: Troll Dung
parts.description: Troll Dung produces a foul odor. If you add it to a potion, the potion smells bad. Did you really need me to spell that out for you? It's troll dung. All about smelliness… Obviously.

parts.name: Troll Skin
parts.description: Not useful in potions. May be useful in either in building, as art (or as a collectible), or in artificing.

creatures.description: Trolls are massive two-legged beasts that often carry clubs.

creatures.name: Leprechaun
creatures.additional_social_rules: Leprechauns can be persuaded by humans by better their human social skills roll.

parts.name: Leprechaun Skin
parts.description: Not useful in potions. May be useful in either in building, as art (or as a collectible), or in artificing.

creatures.description: Leprechaun (sometimes called Clauricorn) are sentient and mischievious beasts. They tend not to be violent. They are sentient but do not have being status.

creatures.name: Merrow
creatures.additional_social_rules: Merrow can be persuaded by humans by bettering their human social skills roll.

parts.name: Merrow Scales
parts.description: Not useful in potions. May be useful in either in building, as art (or as a collectible), or in artificing.

creatures.description: Merrow are a race of merperson. They are cold-water beasts which can be aggressive.

creatures.name: Selkie
creatures.additional_social_rules: Selkie can be persuaded by humans by bettering their human social skills roll.
creatures.description: Selkie are a race of merperson. They are warm-water beasts which can be aggressive.

creatures.name: Phoenix
creatures.additional_social_rules: Phoenix can only be persuaded through bonding.

parts.name: Unmolted Phoenix Skin
parts.description: Not useful in potions. May be useful in either in building, as art (or as a collectible), or in artificing.

parts.name: Phoenix Feathers
parts.description: These feathers are hot to the touch and have strong magical properties. Can be used as a wand core.

creatures.description: Phoenix are powerful and magestic birds. They burn themselves to ash and are reborn every moon cycle. They are fiercely loyal when bonded with strongly. Additionally, they have immense strength, their tears can heal many types of wounds, and can sing a touching song which can heal or harm depending on its intent. The phoenix will only sing this song when it feels particularly moved and will not obey anoother who is attempting to command it to sing.

creatures.name: Hippogriff
creatures.additional_social_rules: Hippogriffs have a very particular expectation for persuasion. They must be shown respect and are quite loyal to those who show them respect.

abilities.name: Mount
abilities.description: This type of creature can be ridden. Unlike with brooms that rely on flying stats for maneuvers, advanced maneuvers when riding creatures will use a creature roll instead.

parts.name: Hippogriff Hide
parts.description: Not useful in potions. May be useful in either in building, as art (or as a collectible), or in artificing.

parts.name: Hippogriff Feathers
parts.description: Not useful in potions. May be useful in either in building, as art (or as a collectible), or in artificing.

creatures.description: Hippogriffs are a hybrid species with an eagle head, the upper torso and forelegs of a bird, the hind legs and tail of a horse. They are winged flight-capable creatures.

creatures.name: Manticore
creatures.additional_social_rules: Manticore cannot be persuaded.

parts.name: Manticore Hide
parts.description: Not useful in potions. May be useful in either in building, as art (or as a collectible), or in artificing.

creatures.description: Manticore are hybrid beasts with an oversized head that appears to have human-like features, a lions body and the tail of a scorpion. They are among the most dangerous creatures known to wizard kind.

creatures.name: Blast Ended Skrewt (Male)
creatures.additional_social_rules: Can be tamed and lured but cannot be bonded with. Tame value must beat a 10.

parts.name: Blast Ended Skrewt Hide
parts.description: Not useful in potions. May be useful in either in building, as art (or as a collectible), or in artificing.

creatures.description: Blast Ended Skrewts are hybrid creatures with features reminicent of fire crabs and manticores. Magical Resistence only works against fire based spells.

creatures.name: Blast Ended Skrewt (Female)
creatures.additional_social_rules: Can be tamed and lured but cannot be bonded with. Tame value must beat a 10.

parts.name: Blast Ended Skrewt Hide
parts.description: Not useful in potions. May be useful in either in building, as art (or as a collectible), or in artificing.

creatures.description: Blast Ended Skrewts are hybrid creatures with features reminicent of fire crabs and manticores. Magical Resistence only works against fire based spells.

creatures.name: Erkling
creatures.additional_social_rules: Erkling are capable of human speech. To convince them you must best their human social skills roll and best a magical creatures roll of 10. Cannot be tamed, bonded with.

parts.name: Erkling Skin
parts.description: Not useful in potions. May be useful in either in building, as art (or as a collectible), or in artificing.

creatures.description: Erklings are small dangerous creatures known to attack children. They use their high pitche cackle to lure children away. When lured, children are unable to run away or fight. During battle, the Erklings must attempt their lure every turn.

creatures.name: Pogrebin
creatures.additional_social_rules: Erkling are capable of human speech. To convince them you must best their human social skills roll and best a magical creatures roll of 10.

attacks.name: Despair
attacks.description: The pogrebin will instill a felt sense of dread and despair in you.

abilities.name: Follow
abilities.description: This allows the Pogrebin to tail you without being detected. Perception must exceed Pogrebin's roll amount.

parts.name: Pogrebin Hair
parts.description: It is believed that pogrebin hair can be poison-enhancing. It is both difficult to acquire and volitile to work with in potions. Any potion using Pogrebin hair requires a potions check of at least 17. Most potions made with pogrebin hair require constant monitoring as well.

parts.name: Pogrebin Skin
parts.description: Not useful in potions. May be useful in either in building, as art (or as a collectible), or in artificing.

creatures.description: Pogrebin are small creatures that resemble rocks with hairly bodies. Pogrebin will quietly trail humans they encounter with the intention of trying to devour them. If their intended prey sits, kneels, or lays down, they will attack to attack and are swift in doing so (ground movement speed = 15; agility = 9). When not attacking, they move according to their default movement statistics. Pogrebin are instantly defeated (though not killed) by using any dark arts spell, any stunning spell, or by kicking them successfully. Their creep attack determines the roll necessary to determine if they are detectable (perception check must better creep attack value).

creatures.name: Hobgoblin
creatures.additional_social_rules: See the main description.

parts.name: Hobgoblin Skin
parts.description: Not useful in potions. May be useful in either in building, as art (or as a collectible), or in artificing.

creatures.description:
Hobgoblins are small, ugly hairy bipedal creatures. Some are very friendly to humans and others are aggressive. The first person the hobgoblin sees must make a social skills roll. If that roll is better than the hobgoblin roll, the hobgoblin will be friendly. The first person the hobgoblin sees must make a social skills roll. If their social skills roll is greater than that of the hobgoblin, the hobgoblin will be friendly. Otherwise it will be adversarial. Hobgoblins may try to distract an opposing wizard, thereby preventing them from taking their turn. Hobgoblins also enjoy playing tricks on their enemies. If their tricks attack is successful, they will do one of four actions:
1. Disarm (steal the wand of other wizard)
2. Trip (knocking the other over)
3. Startle (causing 2L wounds of anxiety damage)
4. Throw mud (causing the embarrassment trait until cleaned)
Hobgoblins can become irritated or annoyed anytime they are unsuccessful at tricking or distracting their opponent. If a hobgoblin is irritated too many times (equal to their Hvy Wound Cap) in its life, it permanently transforms into a boggart. Hobgoblins will not help you fight other hobgoblins.
